summary: Linked additional Metrowerks Standard Library objects by moving several MSL files to Matching, adding missing MSL data objects for FILE and float constants, correcting library symbol names/prototypes, and updating callsites that were using vsprintf-like declarations to the matched vsnprintf signature.
what_changed: Marked src/MSL/ansi_fp.c, src/MSL/printf.c, and src/MSL/trigf.c as Matching in configure.py.
Added new matching MSL objects src/MSL/ansi_files.c and src/MSL/float.c to the MSL object list in configure.py.
Added src/MSL/ansi_files.c defining FILE __files[3] for stdin, stdout, and stderr, with console read/write/close callbacks wired to __read_console, __write_console, and __close_console.
Added src/MSL/float.c containing two integer data constants at the former trigf data addresses: 0x7FFFFFFF and 0x7F800000.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt so ansi_files.c owns data 0x80400430-0x80400508, printf.c data starts at 0x80400508, float.c owns data 0x80400770-0x80400778, and trigf.c data starts at 0x80400778.
Renamed symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt: __num2dec_internal to __num2dec, vsprintf at 0x80323DC8 to vsnprintf, and MSL_ConsoleIo_80325F18 to __close_console.

Updated MSL console I/O declarations and definitions to use signatures closer to MSL callbacks: __write_console(u32, u8*, u32*, void (*)(void)), __read_console(u32, u8*, u32*, void (*)(void)), and __close_console(u32).
Reworked printf.c so printf is implemented before vprintf and uses va_start plus __pformatter directly, while vprintf remains a va_list wrapper around __pformatter.
Trimmed printf.h by removing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS internal declarations and adding a declaration for vsnprintf.
Changed DevText_Printf and HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 callsites from vsprintf to vsnprintf with n = -1 and included printf.h so the corrected MSL prototype is visible.
Added __HI/__LO style macros in ansi_fp.c and __HI in trigf.c, likely to support matching float/double bit access patterns.
smart_moves: Separated data ownership into the correct MSL source files instead of leaving unrelated FILE and float constants attached to printf.c or trigf.c; this made the splits reflect library object boundaries more accurately.
Used symbol renames to align addresses with real MSL API names, especially identifying 0x80323DC8 as vsnprintf rather than vsprintf and 0x80325F18 as __close_console.
Propagated the vsnprintf rename to existing game callsites so source-level declarations, symbol table names, and emitted calls stayed consistent.
Matched the standard MSL FILE table by initializing stdin/stdout/stderr entries with self-referential char_buffer pointers and console callback functions.
Adjusted console I/O prototypes to include the extra callback parameter expected by the FILE function pointers, avoiding mismatched callback types in ansi_files.c.
Kept internal printf helpers private in printf.c by removing unnecessary unknown prototypes from printf.h, reducing header pollution while exposing the newly identified public vsnprintf symbol.
Moved MSL objects to Matching only alongside split/symbol/header fixes, suggesting the PR linked code and data as a coordinated unit rather than toggling status alone.
decomp_lessons: When a library object becomes linkable, update configure.py, splits.txt, symbols.txt, headers, and callsites together; mismatches between these layers can hide the true owning object or function name.
MSL data ranges may need to be split out of neighboring objects once missing source files are added. In this PR, ansi_files.c and float.c carved data out of printf.c and trigf.c respectively.
Do not assume a known-looking formatted-output function is vsprintf solely from behavior. Address 0x80323DC8 was corrected to vsnprintf, and callers were updated to pass an explicit size argument.
For MSL standard streams, FILE initialization can require self-references through stdin/stdout/stderr macros such as taking the address of each stream's char_buffer.
Library callback function pointer signatures matter for matching: console read/write/close prototypes needed a u32 handle and, for read/write, a fourth unused callback parameter.
Real MSL names are preferable to placeholder names once identified; renaming MSL_ConsoleIo_80325F18 to __close_console made its role clear and let ansi_files.c use it naturally.
Bit-level floating-point helpers such as __HI and __LO macros can be local matching tools for MSL math/FP code, but the evidence here only shows macro additions, not the full matching rationale.
naming_conventions: Use real MSL double-underscore names when identified, e.g. __num2dec, __close_console, __read_console, and __write_console.
Correct public formatted-output symbol naming distinguished vsnprintf from vsprintf at address 0x80323DC8.
MSL source files were named after library objects: ansi_files.c for FILE __files data and float.c for float-related constants.
Temporary placeholder-style symbols such as MSL_ConsoleIo_80325F18 were replaced when the standard-library role became known.

printf.h kept public printf-family declarations and avoided exposing internal helper names such as __StringWrite, __FileWrite, __pformatter, float2str, round_decimal, longlong2str, long2str, and parse_format.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py only after adding missing data objects and adjusting split ranges.
Inserted missing MSL data-only or data-heavy source files to make the linker layout match original object boundaries.
Adjusted .data split starts/ends to account for newly separated ansi_files.c and float.c data regions.
Used integer arrays for float bit-pattern constants in src/MSL/float.c to emit exact 32-bit data words 0x7FFFFFFF and 0x7F800000.
Added __HI and __LO pointer-cast macros to match MSL floating-point code that inspects high and low words of floating-point values.
Matched printf/vprintf structure by implementing printf with a local va_list block and routing both printf and vprintf through __pformatter.
Suppressed fortify-source warnings globally with -Wno-fortify-source, likely because corrected printf-family declarations or calls triggered compiler diagnostics under the project toolchain.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment recorded by counts, but no comment body was included in the provided context, so no reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that further rename or validate MSL printf-family functions, especially vsnprintf and vsprintf.
Check future changes to src/MSL/ansi_files.c for struct FILE field naming or replacement of positional initializers with designated fields.
Search for later cleanup of __HI/__LO macros in ansi_fp.c and trigf.c or consolidation with math.h definitions.
Find PRs that convert src/MSL/math.c from NonMatching to Matching after this MSL linking work.
Search whether -Wno-fortify-source was later removed or refined once printf prototypes stabilized.
